steps.title: Identify the criterion for suitability
steps.content: The question asks which species is “least suited” to full migration to the mesophotic zone (30–150 meters). The key signal is that a species currently found only in shallow water has never adapted to the conditions there. Therefore, the species with the shallowest maximum depth—furthest from 30 meters—would be least suited.

steps.title: Apply the criterion to the data
steps.content: Acropora anthocercis has a maximum depth of only 10 meters—furthest from the 30-meter threshold—so it's never experienced mesophotic conditions. The others have either greater maximum depths or minimum depths indicating deeper ranges. That matches choice B — B is correct.
